name: AWS.Auth

unions.name: Challenge
unions.comment:
The types of challenges that Cognito can issue.

Challenge types not yet covered:

  - SmsMfa
  - SoftwareTokenMfa
  - SelectMfaType
  - MfaSetup
  - PasswordVerifier
  - CustomChallenge
  - DeviceSrpAuth
  - DevicePasswordVerifier
  - AdminNoSrpAuth

aliases.name: AuthExtensions
aliases.comment:
Defines the extensions to the `AuthAPI.Authenticated` fields that this
authenticator supports.

aliases.name: CognitoAPI
aliases.comment:
AWS Cognito specific API for:

  - Responding to challenges.
  - Obtaining temporary AWS access credentials (for signing requests).
aliases.type: { requiredNewPassword : String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd AWS.Auth.Msg, getAWSCredentials : AWS.Auth.Model -> Maybe.Maybe AWS.Core.Credentials.Credentials }

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
The configuration needed to interact with Cognito.

The `userIdentityMapping` field is optional. Fill it in and a request to obtain
AWS credentials will be automatically made once logged in. That is to say that
the user will be mapped to an AWS IAM identity, which can be used to access
AWS services directly through request signing.
aliases.type: { clientId : String.String, region : AWS.Core.Service.Region, userIdentityMapping : Maybe.Maybe AWS.Auth.UserIdentityMappingConfig }

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment: The authentication model consisting of the evaluated config and the private state.
aliases.type: { clientId : AWS.CognitoIdentityProvider.ClientIdType, region : AWS.Core.Service.Region, userIdentityMapping : Maybe.Maybe AWS.Auth.UserIdentityMapping, innerModel : AWS.Auth.Private }

values.name: api
values.comment:
An extended API for working with Cognito.

This provides the functions needed to response to Cognito challenges.
values.type: AuthAPI.AuthAPI AWS.Auth.Config AWS.Auth.Model AWS.Auth.Msg AWS.Auth.AuthExtensions AWS.Auth.Challenge AWS.Auth.CognitoAPI
